target.title: fix(status): do not promote cumulative transcript usage as Context numerator (#83526)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #83526 — the TUI / `/status` Context line could render percentages
greater than 100% (e.g. `2.3m/1.0m (228%)`) even with `Compactions: 0` and
no actual context overflow.

## Root cause

`readUsageFromSessionLog` (in `src/status/status-message.ts`) returns a
`promptTokens` value used as the Context-window numerator. When the
transcript has no per-turn `totalTokens` field, `promptTokens` falls back
to `input + cacheRead + cacheWrite`. But `extractAggregateUsageFromTranscriptLines`
sums those fields across **every** transcript line, so the fallback is
cumulative session usage, not the current prompt size.

`buildStatusMessage` then promoted that cumulative value into `totalTokens`
whenever it exceeded the session entry's value:

```ts
const candidate = logUsage.promptTokens || logUsage.total;
if (allowTranscriptContextUsage && (!totalTokens || candidate > totalTokens)) {
  totalTokens = candidate;
}
```

On long cache-heavy sessions the candidate is dominated by repeated
`cacheRead`, easily exceeding the 1M context window.

## Fix

- `readUsageFromSessionLog` now reports a `promptTokensFresh` flag,
  `true` only when the snapshot's `totalTokens` came from a fresh per-turn
  record (the same freshness signal `extractAggregateUsageFromTranscriptLines`
  already sets via `snapshot.totalTokensFresh = true`).
- The promotion site in `buildStatusMessage` requires that flag before
  treating the transcript value as the Context numerator.
- Aggregate `input` / `output` / `cacheRead` / `cacheWrite` continue to
  hydrate the separate Tokens and Cache lines — only the Context %
  numerator is gated.

This keeps the existing transcript-driven Context % working for fresh
per-turn snapshots (covered by `reads transcript usage for non-default
agents` and `renders CLI runtime aliases as the selected model route`)
and the existing `entry?.totalTokensFresh !== false` gate continues to
suppress stale entries.

## Test

Adds `does not render cumulative cache reads as context usage on legacy
sessions` in `src/auto-reply/status.test.ts`, which reproduces the
reported case: a legacy session entry (`totalTokensFresh: undefined`) plus
a 5-turn transcript whose lines lack per-turn `totalTokens` but carry
heavy `cacheRead`. The test asserts the Context display never renders a
numerator that exceeds the window (no `(\d{3,}%)`, no `2.3m/1.0m`).

## Verification (run in an isolated `node:24-bookworm` container)

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/status.test.ts                       72/72  ✅
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/session-store.test.ts \
                            src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts               298/298 ✅
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/status/status-message.test.ts                    2/2   ✅
```

(The first three files are the acceptance criteria called out in the
ClawSweeper review on the issue.)

## Related

- #55217 (closed) — same root cause, fixed only for Web UI.
- #72140 — adjacent: post-compaction stale read.
- #39857 — adjacent: context window over-reporting.

## Test plan

- [x] New regression test in `src/auto-reply/status.test.ts` reproduces the bug pre-fix and passes post-fix.
- [x] `src/auto-reply/status.test.ts` — all 72 tests pass.
- [x] `src/agents/command/session-store.test.ts` + `src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts` — all 298 tests pass.
- [x] `src/status/status-message.test.ts` — passes.
- [ ] Maintainer smoke-test against a real long cache-heavy Claude CLI session.
target.author: therahul-yo
